definitions: To cover a surface with pavement, typically asphalt or concrete, often to replace natural ground or an existing structure.
To conceal or suppress something, especially a problem or scandal, by creating a superficial appearance of normalcy or resolution.
examples: They decided to pave over the old tennis courts to create more parking.
The city council voted to pave over the last remaining green space.
The company tried to pave over the scandal with a public relations campaign.
